conversation.text: Hey, just set up SpendYes. I'm an engineering manager in Seattle, currently using it to replace Mint and YNAB. Can we start with the basics — can you show me what my current balance is and help me set up my biweekly pay schedule?
This is just for me — so I can spot your rhythm and nudge in the moment. No bank link, nothing shared. What's roughly in your account?
Right, got it. I've got about $4,870 in checking right now. And for pay — I get $11,000 biweekly, every other Friday. Can you set that up so I can track against it?

All set ✅ — $4,870.00, payday 2026-05-08.

From here just talk to me: "spent 20 on coffee", "rent 1400 due the 1st", "can I afford 200?" — I'll handle it.

🟢 *Calm* — $695.71/day, 7 days to payday.
Logging your $11k biweekly paycheck — next one 2026-05-08. You're all set to track.

Payday → 2026-05-08 (biweekly)  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Payday → 2026-05-08 (biweekly)
🟢 *Calm* — $695.71/day, 7 days to payday.

Great. Now let me ask — can you handle fixed bills? I've got mortgage due the 1st ($2,450), car payment on the 12th ($385), and internet on the 20th ($90). Can I set those up so you know what's committed each month?

Adding mortgage, car, and internet — $2,925/month committed. You'll have ~$1,945/month after bills to work with.

*I'll add:*
  1. Add bill *Mortgage* — $2,450.00 · 2026-05-01 · monthly
  2. Add bill *Car payment* — $385.00 · 2026-05-12 · monthly
  3. Add bill *Internet* — $90.00 · 2026-05-20 · monthly  [buttons: Yes, all 3 / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes, all 3)

(edit) ✓ Add bill *Mortgage* — $2,450.00 · 2026-05-01 · monthly
✓ Add bill *Car payment* — $385.00 · 2026-05-12 · monthly
✓ Add bill *Internet* — $90.00 · 2026-05-20 · monthly
🟢 *Calm* — $345.71/day, 7 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 7 days ($345.71/day).

Good, that matches what I expected. Now — can you handle spending categories? I want to track groceries, gas, and kid stuff separately so I can see where the money's actually going each month. And can you set limits on any of those?

I can't do category budgets or spending limits yet — but I *can* log every spend with a note ("$60 on groceries", "$45 on gas", "$30 on kid stuff") and then show you trends over time. That'll give you the visibility you need. Want to start logging spends that way?
(tapped: Undo: bill Internet)

Undone.
🟢 *Calm* — $345.71/day, 7 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 7 days ($345.71/day).

Okay, got it — so I can log with notes but no hard category limits yet. That's fair. Let me start tracking then. But before I do — can you connect to my bank account so transactions pull in automatically? Or do I need to manually log everything?

I can't connect to your bank yet — you'll need to log manually for now. But it's quick: just tell me "spent 60 on groceries" and I'll record it. Once you start, patterns will show up in a few days and I'll flag the big leaks.

Got it — manual logging for now, no bank sync yet. That's fine, I've done that with other apps before. Let me ask one more thing though: can you show me spending trends over time? Like, what did I spend on groceries last month vs. this month, or gas over the last 3 months?

Alright, that's helpful. So 30-day trends for now, got it. I can work with that. Let me start logging and see what the data looks like. Should I just text you like "$62.50 on groceries" or is there a specific format you prefer?

Cool, I'll start logging then. Just to confirm — when I log a spend, does it hit my balance right away? And can I edit or delete a transaction after I log it, like if I made a mistake or want to change the category note?

Yep, spend hits your balance right away. If you make a mistake, I can undo your last action — but I can't edit or delete specific old transactions yet. So double-check before you send, or just undo and re-log if needed.

Got it — so undo works for the last action, but no editing specific past transactions yet. That's a limitation I can work around. Alright, let me start logging some expenses and see how it goes. I'll reach out if I hit a wall or need something else.
Perfect — I'm ready when you are. Log spends as you go, and I'll start spotting patterns in a few days. Good luck!

Sounds good. One last thing before I dive in — I have a biweekly paycheck every other Friday, and I'm trying to build a college fund for my kid. Can I set up an automated transfer so a fixed amount goes there each payday? Or do I need to manually move money around?

I can't do automated transfers to other accounts yet — that's outside my toolkit for now. But I can update your payday to every other Friday so the math stays tight. For the college fund, you'd need to move it manually after each paycheck, or use your bank's transfer tool. Want me to lock in your payday frequency?
Yeah, that makes sense — I can handle the manual transfers. Go ahead and set my payday to every other Friday though. That'll help me plan around the mortgage and car payment due dates.

Locking in your payday for every other Friday. That'll help you see the breathing room between paydays and your bills.

Payday → 2026-05-08 (biweekly)  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Payday → 2026-05-08 (biweekly)
🟢 *Calm* — $345.71/day, 7 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 7 days ($345.71/day).

Thanks for setting that up. So just to clarify — the $345.71/day burn rate, that's based on my current balance minus the mortgage that's due today, right? And does that daily rate update once I start logging expenses, or is it just a static projection?

Good questions. The $345.71/day is your post-bills runway ($2,420 after mortgage and car payment) divided by 7 days until payday — it updates every time you log a spend or income. So it'll shift as your balance changes and as bills get marked paid.
(tapped: Undo: payday change)

Undone.
🟢 *Calm* — $345.71/day, 7 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 7 days ($345.71/day).

Wait, hold on — I just undid the payday change, but the screen still shows the same $345.71/day burn rate and 7 days to payday. Did that actually revert, or is it still set to biweekly? Can you confirm what my payday frequency is right now?

Good catch — I don't have visibility into whether the undo worked on the backend. Your STATE still shows payday on 2026-05-08 (7 days away), but I can't confirm the frequency from here. Best move: try setting it again to biweekly, and if it sticks this time we'll know the undo went through. Want me to update it?
Yeah, that's frustrating — I need to know for sure. Go ahead and set it back to biweekly. If it takes this time, we'll know the undo didn't work the first time.

Setting your payday to biweekly starting 2026-05-08. This time it should stick.

Payday → 2026-05-08 (biweekly)  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Payday → 2026-05-08 (biweekly)
🟢 *Calm* — $345.71/day, 7 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 7 days ($345.71/day).

Okay, good. So that's set now. Before I move on — can you also show me a breakdown of my spending by category over the last month? I want to see where the groceries, gas, and kid stuff are adding up. I know YNAB and Copilot both do that pretty easily.

I can't do category breakdowns yet — but I can help you tag your spends with notes like 'groceries' or 'gas' as you log them, so we start building that picture. Once you've logged a few, I'll spot your biggest leaks. Want to start logging some recent expenses?
(tapped: Undo: payday change)

Undone.
🟢 *Calm* — $345.71/day, 7 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 7 days ($345.71/day).

Hold on — I just undid the payday change again, but the burn rate and countdown haven't changed at all. It still says $345.71/day and 7 days to payday. That doesn't make sense. Are you actually processing the undo, or is this a display bug?

You're right — that's weird. I'm emitting the undo, but the STATE isn't changing, which means either the undo isn't reversing the payday update on the backend, or there's a display lag. This is a bug I can't diagnose from here. Can you refresh the screen or try a different action (like logging a small spend) to see if the state updates? If it's still stuck at 2026-05-08 after that, we may need to manually correct it.

Yeah, this is exactly the kind of thing that kills trust. I've used enough apps to know when something's actually broken versus a display issue, and this feels like a real backend problem. Let me refresh and see what happens.
Fair point — that's frustrating. Refresh and let me know what you see. If the payday is still stuck at 2026-05-08, we can force-correct it with adjust_balance or a manual payday reset. I'm here.

Okay, refreshed. Still showing 2026-05-08 and $345.71/day. This is definitely a backend issue, not a display lag. Can you force-correct it? I need the payday to go back to what it was before I messed with it.
I need to know: what was your payday *before* you changed it to biweekly? Once you tell me the original date and frequency, I can force-correct it with update_payday.
It was every other Friday — so the next one should be this Friday, May 10th. Then the one after that is May 24th. Can you reset it to that pattern?

Resetting payday to Friday, May 10th on a biweekly schedule.

Payday → 2026-05-10 (biweekly)  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Payday → 2026-05-10 (biweekly)
🟢 *Calm* — $268.88/day, 9 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 9 days ($268.88/day).

Okay, that's better. The math checks out now — $268.88/day with 9 days to payday makes sense. Thanks for fixing that. So I'm good until Friday. Can we move on to something else, or do you want me to test anything else while we're here?
Good — payday locked back to May 10th. You're solid. What's next?
(tapped: Undo: payday change)

Undone.
🟢 *Calm* — $345.71/day, 7 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 7 days ($345.71/day).
